_meta.discipline: Records excluded from Discovery Engine v2.0 (discover_primary_source_parallels) because they are CORPUS ARTIFACTS — colophon-type prototypes, format templates, or other meta-records that aggregate standardized language across many manuscripts rather than representing individual tablets. Sign-trigram parallels involving these records generate systematic false positives (the v0.13.4 calibration pass surfaced the colophon-template false-positive class explicitly). Loaded by scripts/discovery-primary-v2.mjs and applied at index-build time so excluded records never enter the candidate pool.
exclusion_categories: colophon_template_prototype — Ashurbanipal-colophon type prototypes (Hunger 1968 BAK Types A-Z) that aggregate standardized AN.ŠÁR-DÙ-A É.GAL colophon vocabulary across 100-200 manuscripts each. Their trigram-similarity to any Kuyunjik tablet reflects formulaic colophon-language match, not meaningful intertextual content.
_meta.discovered_by: Discovery Engine v0.13.4 mid-tier validation pass (2026-05-15) — K.3716 ↔ Asb.d and K.3716 ↔ Asb.c surfaced as cross-genre 'parallels' between an unidentified Kuyunjik fragment and what turned out to be colophon-type prototypes (Asb.d = 116 manuscripts; Asb.c = 212 manuscripts; per eBL editor notes 'SC'). These are formulaic artifacts, not true intertextual parallels. v0.14.4 closes the loop by excluding all 20 Asb.* prototype records from the discovery corpus.
_meta.scholarly_anchor: Hunger, H. 1968. *Babylonische und assyrische Kolophone*. AOAT 2. Neukirchen-Vluyn. The canonical typology of Mesopotamian colophons. The 'Asb' prefix denotes Ashurbanipal-library colophon types A through Z; eBL records each type as a virtual record aggregating the standardized colophon language across all manuscripts of that type.

excluded_records.rationale: Hunger 1968 BAK Type-A Ashurbanipal-library palace colophon prototype. Not an individual tablet.
Hunger 1968 BAK Type-B Ashurbanipal-library palace colophon prototype.

excluded_records.manuscript_count_source: eBL editor note SC: '212 records: 54% magic, 4% medicine, 1% literature, 1% lexicography, 0% astrology, 0% hemerology?, 39% uncertain'
excluded_records.rationale: Hunger 1968 BAK Type-C Ashurbanipal-library palace colophon. Aggregates colophon language across 212 manuscripts. v0.13.4 first identified the false-positive class via the K.3716 ↔ Asb.c parallel.

excluded_records.manuscript_count_source: eBL editor note: '116 records: Literature 16%, Lexicography 15%, Magic 11%, Hymn 7%, Astrology 5%, Terrestrial 5%, Medicine (diagnostics) 4%, Teratological texts 4%, Physiognomy 3%, historiography 3%, Hemerology 1%, Kalûtu 1%, Catalogue 1%, Astronomy 1%, Unclear 22%'
excluded_records.rationale: Hunger 1968 BAK Type-D Ashurbanipal-library palace colophon. Aggregates colophon language across 116 manuscripts.
